data.description:
## Why
Contamination from Pass-1 bin-packing is currently UNMEASURABLE: the sidecar stamps each findings criteria but throws away chunk co-residency (only a chunk COUNT survives). You cannot calibrate the cost/rigor tradeoff (R-1 part 2, epic fort-wisp-wren) without measuring it first.

## What
In pass1_chunk (passes.py:294-306) stamp each emitted finding with its chunk COHORT (the sorted criterion-id set of its chunk); carry that one key through _slim (sidecar.py:236-264). Then "finding F on criterion A co-resided with {B,C}" becomes a direct offline read over accumulated sidecar data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each Pass-1 finding carries its cohort (sorted chunk criterion-id set) in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar
- [ ] Offline analysis can measure blocking-tier contamination rate
- [ ] Unblocks the evidence gate for fort-wisp-wren (R-1 part 2)
data.title: WS9: Sidecar cohort observability field (R-1 part 1)
